response:
# Animated Page Transition Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, motion-sensitive users, low vision users > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, keyboard-only navigation **Save plan to:** `docs/a11y-plans/2026-06-13-animated-page-transition-a11y-plan.md` **Feature:** React SPA route transition wrapper using framer-motion slide and fade variants, with reduced-motion support and post-navigation focus management. **Risk Level:** Moderate **Component/Page Type:** Route transition wrapper / SPA navigation infrastructure --- ## Scope & Context This plan covers the `PageTransition` wrapper applied to all React Router v6 routes. The component animates route changes with framer-motion: - Default page routes: outgoing page slides left, incoming page slides in from right, 300ms. - Modal-like overlay routes: cross-fade transition, 200ms. - Route change is instant, with no server fetch delay. - Existing implementation uses `AnimatePresence` but has no reduced-motion handling and no post-navigation focus management. The accessibility risks are not primarily semantic complexity. The risks are: 1. Motion-triggered vestibular discomfort from slide transitions. 2. Keyboard or screen reader focus remaining on content that has visually exited. 3. `AnimatePresence` keeping outgoing content in the DOM during exit animation, allowing assistive tech to encounter stale content. 4. Silent route changes in the SPA because browser page-load announcements do not happen automatically. This plan does not cover in-page micro-animations, loading states, network delays, or individual route content accessibility. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan Each route must expose one clear main content target for focus and screen reader orientation. ### Required Route Structure Each rendered route should include: ```html

Current Page Title

...
``` ### Semantic Requirements | Structure | Requirement | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---| | `
` landmark | Each route has one primary content region | WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks | | `

` page title | Each route has a meaningful first heading | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels | | `tabindex="-1"` on `
` | Allows programmatic focus after navigation without adding `
` to normal tab order | WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order | | `aria-labelledby="page-title"` | Gives the focused main landmark a useful accessible name | WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value | | Skip link remains first focusable item | Keyboard users can bypass repeated navigation | WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks | ### Structure Diagram ```html Skip to main content
...

Route title

...
...
``` The exact wrapper elements may differ in React, but the accessibility contract must hold: one current route is exposed to assistive technology, exiting routes are hidden/inert, and focus lands on the new route’s main content. --- ## Interaction Pattern Design There is no APG “page transition” pattern. This component uses native SPA navigation plus focus and status-message patterns. ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Route links | Native Link / APG Link behavior | Tab moves to link; Enter activates route navigation | Native anchor/link accessible name from text; avoid `title` as name | WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.4 Link Purpose, 4.1.2 | | Current route container | Landmark / document structure, no APG widget | Not tabbable by default; receives programmatic focus after navigation | `
` | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 | | Route announcer | Status message pattern | Not focusable | `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"` | WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages | | Exiting route layer | Hidden/inert content strategy | Must not receive focus during exit animation | `aria-hidden="true"` and `inert` while exiting | WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.2 | | Transition wrapper | No interactive role | No keyboard interaction | No custom role unless needed | WCAG 4.1.2 | ### APG / Pattern Mapping - **Route links:** Native HTML link behavior. Do not replace links with clickable `div`s. If React Router uses link components, they must render accessible anchor semantics. Citation: WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.4 Link Purpose, 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. - **Route announcement:** Status message pattern using a polite live region. Citation: WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages. - **Post-navigation focus:** SPA focus management pattern. Focus must move to the new page’s `
` or first heading after route change. Citation: WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels. - **Reduced motion:** User-triggered animation must respect motion preferences. Use framer-motion’s `useReducedMotion()` hook. Citation: WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions. --- ## Keyboard Interaction Model The transition itself must not introduce custom keyboard behavior. Route navigation remains native: - `Tab`: moves through navigation links and route content in logical DOM order. - `Enter`: activates focused route link. - `Space`: no special behavior for links; native buttons elsewhere retain native behavior. - `Escape`: no behavior for normal page transitions. Overlay routes may use Escape only if the overlay itself is implemented as a modal/dialog pattern. - Arrow keys: no transition-level behavior. After a route link is activated with keyboard: 1. Route changes. 2. Animation begins or reduced-motion alternative runs. 3. Exiting route is removed from the accessibility tree immediately. 4. Focus moves to the new route’s `
` or first `

`. 5. A polite route announcement is made. Focus must not remain on the clicked navigation link if that creates ambiguity about whether navigation completed. Focus must not remain on an element inside the outgoing page. --- ## Focus Management Plan ### Primary Strategy After every route change, programmatically focus the new route’s `
` element: ```html
``` This gives screen reader and keyboard users a clear starting point in the new page without inserting the landmark into the normal tab sequence. ### Focus Timing Focus should move after React has rendered the incoming route content, not after the full animation completes. Reason: the route change is instant and content is available immediately. Waiting 300ms creates a period where focus may remain on old or ambiguous content. Recommended timing: 1. Detect location change from React Router. 2. Render incoming route. 3. On layout/effect after render, focus the incoming route’s `
`. 4. Update document title and live region text. ### Focus Target Priority Use this order: 1. The route’s `
` if present. 2. The route’s `

` if `
` cannot receive focus. 3. A fallback route container with `tabindex="-1"` and accessible name. The preferred target is `
` because it establishes page context. The `

` fallback is acceptable when the page content architecture cannot guarantee a stable main ref. ### Focus Restoration This is route navigation, not a modal close. Do not restore focus to the triggering link after navigation. Restoration to trigger is appropriate for temporary overlays and dialogs, but not for committed page navigation. For modal-like overlay routes, focus behavior depends on the overlay pattern: - If the overlay route behaves like a modal dialog, it must follow the APG Modal Dialog pattern separately: focus moves into the dialog, focus is trapped, Escape closes, and focus restores to the opener. - If the overlay route is just a visually faded route transition, focus still moves to the new route’s `
`. This plan is not claiming all overlay routes are modals. The fade variant alone does not create modal semantics. ### DOM Overlap During AnimatePresence `AnimatePresence` can keep outgoing content mounted while exit animation runs. During that period: - The exiting route layer must be removed from the accessibility tree using `aria-hidden="true"`. - The exiting route layer must be made unfocusable using `inert`. - If `inert` support/polyfill is unavailable, fallback must remove all focusable descendants from tab order during exit or conditionally render only the current route for assistive technology. `aria-hidden="true"` alone is not enough because focusable descendants can still receive keyboard focus. `inert` is needed to prevent focus access. WCAG citations: - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order: focus must not move to or remain on content that is no longer the current page. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value: hidden/current route state must be programmatically coherent. ### Skip Link The existing skip link must continue targeting the current route’s active `
`. If duplicate `id="main-content"` nodes exist during exit animation, that is a failure. During DOM overlap, only the current route may expose `id="main-content"` to navigation and assistive tech. The exiting route should either: - Be hidden/inert before the incoming route becomes current, or - Have its IDs scoped/removed while exiting to avoid duplicate active IDs. --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA Attribute | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Current route entering/current | Incoming page visible and becomes stable | Exposed to accessibility tree; focusable `
` receives focus | `
` | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 | | Exiting route | Slides/fades away visually | Removed from accessibility tree and focus order | `aria-hidden="true"` plus `inert` on exiting wrapper | WCAG 2.4.3, 4.1.2 | | Route changed | New page title visible | Polite live region announces new page title | `aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true"` | WCAG 4.1.3 | | Reduced motion enabled | No slide motion; instant switch or short fade | Motion preference read from system | framer-motion `useReducedMotion()` controls variants | WCAG 2.3.3 | | Standard slide transition | 300ms horizontal transform | No ARIA required; content exposure still managed | None for animation itself | WCAG 2.3.3, 2.2.2 | | Fade overlay transition | 200ms opacity cross-fade | No ARIA required unless route is a modal | None for animation itself | WCAG 2.3.3, 2.2.2 | | Navigation complete | Page content stable | Document title updated and route announcer updated | Live region text update | WCAG 2.4.2 Page Titled, 4.1.3 | ### Route Announcer Add one global live region, not one per page or per route component: ```html
``` On route change, update it with a concise message: - “Navigated to Settings” - “Search Results” - “Account Details” Do not use `role="alert"` for normal navigation. Route changes are important but not urgent interruptions. WCAG citation: 4.1.3 Status Messages. ### Document Title Update `document.title` on every route change. Example format: - `Settings | Product Name` - `Search Results | Product Name` WCAG citation: 2.4.2 Page Titled. ### ARIA Attribute Rules - `aria-live="polite"`: announces non-urgent route completion. WCAG 4.1.3. - `aria-atomic="true"`: ensures the complete route announcement is read. WCAG 4.1.3. - `aria-hidden="true"` on exiting layer: prevents stale page content from being read. WCAG 4.1.2. - `inert` on exiting layer: prevents keyboard focus into stale content. WCAG 2.4.3. - `aria-labelledby` on `
`: ties the landmark to the page heading. WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2. - `tabindex="-1"` on `
`: supports programmatic focus without changing tab order. WCAG 2.4.3. --- ## Visual Accessibility Plan ### Motion Reduction Use framer-motion’s `useReducedMotion()` hook as the source of truth for the user’s system preference. Reduced motion must not mean “broken transition” or blank intermediate state. It must provide a stable alternative. ### Transition Variants | Variant | Standard Motion | Reduced-Motion Alternative | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---| | Slide page transition | 300ms transform: outgoing left, incoming from right | Instant route switch, or opacity-only fade ≤150ms with no transform | WCAG 2.3.3 | | Fade overlay transition | 200ms opacity cross-dissolve | Instant switch, or opacity fade ≤150ms | WCAG 2.3.3 | Preferred reduced-motion behavior: - Slide variant: instant switch, no transform. - Fade variant: ≤150ms opacity fade is acceptable if it does not create vestibular discomfort. Do not use horizontal movement, parallax, scale, zoom, rotation, bounce, spring overshoot, or simulated depth in reduced-motion mode. ### Animation Duration The standard 300ms slide and 200ms fade are short. They do not trigger WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide because that criterion applies to moving/blinking/scrolling content that starts automatically and lasts more than five seconds, or auto-updating content. However, duration compliance is not enough. WCAG 2.3.3 still applies because the animation is triggered by user navigation and can cause vestibular discomfort. The system preference must be respected. ### Focus Indicators During Transition Focus indicators must remain visible and not be obscured by transition wrappers. - Focused `
` may have a visually hidden or subtle focus style if focusing the landmark would create a large outline, but there must be no loss of keyboard focus visibility for actual interactive controls. - Interactive controls must use visible `:focus-visible` styling. - Focus indicators must meet 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors. WCAG citations: - WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible. - WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured. - WCAG 2.4.13 Focus Appearance. ### Color and Contrast The transition itself should not rely on color to communicate state. If overlay routes dim or fade the background, ensure foreground content maintains: - 4.5:1 contrast for normal text. - 3:1 contrast for large text and non-text UI components. WCAG citations: 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum, 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast. --- ## Content Accessibility Plan ### Page Titles and Headings Every route must provide: - A meaningful document title. - A visible `

`. - A stable accessible route name used by the announcer. The route announcement should match or closely mirror the visible page heading. Do not announce vague messages like “Page loaded” without naming the destination. WCAG citations: 2.4.2 Page Titled, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels. ### Reading Order DOM order must match the route’s visual reading order. The animation may visually move pages, but it must not reorder meaningful content in a way that changes screen reader order. WCAG citation: 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence. ### Hidden Exiting Content Outgoing content must not be readable after navigation begins. This prevents screen reader users from encountering both old and new pages during the animation overlap. Required strategy: - Current route: accessible. - Exiting route: `aria-hidden="true"` and `inert`. - Removed route: unmounted after exit animation. WCAG citations: 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. --- ## Testing Strategy ### Automated Runtime Testing Run axe-core against: - Standard slide route transition. - Fade overlay route transition. - Reduced-motion enabled. - Reduced-motion disabled. - During and after route transition if test tooling can pause animation state. Expected checks: - No duplicate active `main-content` IDs. - One exposed `
` landmark for the current route. - No focusable elements inside `aria-hidden` exiting route. - Valid ARIA attributes. - Page has meaningful heading structure. Axe will not fully verify focus movement, live region announcement quality, or vestibular safety. Manual and keyboard tests are required. ### DOM Verification DOM tests must verify: - Exiting route wrapper receives `aria-hidden="true"`. - Exiting route wrapper receives `inert`. - Incoming/current route does not receive `aria-hidden` or `inert`. - Only one active `#main-content` target exists. - The route announcer exists once globally. - `document.activeElement` becomes the new route’s `
` or fallback heading after navigation. - `document.title` updates. ### Keyboard Tests Test with real keyboard navigation: 1. Tab to a route link. 2. Press Enter. 3. Verify route changes. 4. Verify focus moves to new `
`. 5. Press Tab. 6. Verify focus moves to the first meaningful interactive element in the new page, not the outgoing page. 7. Shift+Tab from the first control and verify focus order remains logical. 8. Repeat for slide and fade variants. 9. Repeat with reduced motion enabled. ### Screen Reader Tests Test with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver: - Activate a route link. - Confirm the new page title or heading is announced. - Confirm the old page is not read during the exit animation. - Confirm the current route’s main landmark and heading are discoverable. - Confirm repeated rapid navigations do not produce confusing stacked announcements. Expected announcement pattern: - Link activation occurs. - Focus lands on main content. - Screen reader announces the main landmark or heading. - Live region politely announces the route name if focus announcement alone is insufficient. ### Reduced Motion Tests Simulate `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` using browser devtools or Playwright media emulation. Acceptance criteria: - Slide transform is not used. - No horizontal movement occurs. - Reduced slide uses instant transition or ≤150ms opacity-only fade. - Fade variant uses instant transition or ≤150ms opacity-only fade. - Focus management still works. - Route announcer still works. - Exiting content is still hidden/inert. ### Visual Regression Tests Verify: - No blank page flash during instant reduced-motion transition. - Focus indicators remain visible after navigation. - Layout does not shift in a way that obscures focused content. - At 200% zoom, focused main content and first interactive element remain visible. - Sticky headers do not cover the focused heading or main content. --- ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Add Reduced-Motion-Aware Transition Variants 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - `PageTransition` component - Route transition variant configuration - Transition tests **Structure Stub:** ```html
...
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - None required for animation itself. - Animation state must not be communicated with ARIA unless it affects content availability. **Implementation Requirements:** - Use framer-motion `useReducedMotion()`. - If reduced motion is true: - Slide variant becomes instant route switch or opacity-only fade ≤150ms. - Fade variant becomes instant route switch or opacity-only fade ≤150ms. - Do not use transform movement in reduced-motion mode. - Preserve designer durations only for no-preference mode: 300ms slide, 200ms fade. **Keyboard Interactions:** - No custom transition keyboard handling. - Native route links remain keyboard accessible. **Tests:** - Reduced motion enabled removes transform-based slide. - Reduced motion disabled preserves 300ms slide. - Fade variant remains supported. - Route content remains present and usable after transition. **WCAG Criteria:** - WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions. - WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide, documented as not triggered by 200-300ms transitions but still relevant as a duration boundary. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard. **a11y-Critic Focus Areas:** - Confirm reduced-motion alternative is real, not just undocumented. - Confirm no transform motion remains in reduced-motion mode. - Confirm framer-motion hook is used consistently for both variants. --- ### Task 2: Hide and Inert Exiting Routes During AnimatePresence Overlap 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - `PageTransition` component - Transition layer wrapper - DOM tests **Structure Stub:** ```html
...
...
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-hidden="true"` on exiting route wrapper: removes stale content from accessibility tree. WCAG 4.1.2. - `inert` on exiting route wrapper: prevents focus into stale content. WCAG 2.4.3. - Do not put `aria-hidden="true"` on the current route. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Tab must never enter exiting content. - Shift+Tab must never enter exiting content. - Existing focus inside outgoing route must be replaced by focus on incoming route. **Tests:** - During exit animation, querying focusable descendants of exiting route shows they cannot receive focus. - Exiting route has `aria-hidden="true"` and `inert`. - Current route does not have `aria-hidden` or `inert`. - No duplicate active `main-content` target is exposed. **WCAG Criteria:** - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. - WCAG 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence. **a11y-Critic Focus Areas:** - Verify `aria-hidden` is not used without `inert`. - Verify outgoing content cannot be reached by keyboard. - Verify current route remains exposed to screen readers. --- ### Task 3: Add Post-Navigation Focus Management 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - `PageTransition` component or route focus manager - Route layout component - Focus tests **Structure Stub:** ```html

Route title

``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `tabindex="-1"` on `
`: enables programmatic focus. WCAG 2.4.3. - `aria-labelledby="page-title"` on `
`: gives the focused region a useful accessible name. WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2. **Keyboard Interactions:** - After keyboard route activation, focus moves to the new page’s `
`. - Pressing Tab after focus lands on `
` moves to the first interactive element in the new page. - Focus does not return to the triggering route link for normal navigation. **Tests:** - Activate route link with Enter. - Verify `document.activeElement` is the new route’s `
`. - Verify next Tab reaches new page content. - Verify outgoing page controls are skipped. - Repeat for slide, fade, and reduced-motion variants. **WCAG Criteria:** - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. - WCAG 2.4.6 Headings and Labels. - WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. **a11y-Critic Focus Areas:** - Verify focus timing is after render and not delayed until after animation unnecessarily. - Verify focus target has a meaningful accessible name. - Verify focus does not land on hidden or outgoing content. --- ### Task 4: Add Route Title and Live Announcement Support 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - App shell/layout - Route metadata configuration - Route announcer component - Screen reader behavior tests/manual test script **Structure Stub:** ```html
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-live="polite"`: announces non-urgent route changes. WCAG 4.1.3. - `aria-atomic="true"`: announces the full route message. WCAG 4.1.3. **Keyboard Interactions:** - No focusable controls added. - Announcer must not be tabbable. **Tests:** - Route change updates `document.title`. - Route change updates live region text. - Live region exists once globally. - Message includes the destination page name. - Rapid route changes do not stack stale announcements. **WCAG Criteria:** - WCAG 2.4.2 Page Titled. - WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages. - WCAG 2.4.6 Headings and Labels. **a11y-Critic Focus Areas:** - Verify live region is global, not duplicated per route. - Verify announcements are concise and destination-specific. - Verify route title, `

`, and announcement do not contradict each other. --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas | |---|---|---| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Reduced-motion behavior, framer-motion `useReducedMotion()`, no transform motion in reduced mode | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Exiting route `aria-hidden` plus `inert`, no stale content in focus order or accessibility tree | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Post-navigation focus target, focus timing, logical Tab order after route change | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Route title updates, polite live announcement, no duplicate announcers | --- ## Architecture Overview The implementation should consist of: - One `PageTransition` wrapper using framer-motion `AnimatePresence`. - One reduced-motion-aware transition variant system. - One route focus manager that focuses the active route’s `
`. - One global route announcer using `aria-live="polite"`. - Route layout requirements requiring `
`. The central accessibility contract: only the current route is exposed and focusable; motion respects user preference; route changes are announced and focused like meaningful page navigation. --- ## Failure Modes This plan prevents these specific failures: - Slide animation continues for users with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. - Focus remains on a link or control from the outgoing page. - Screen reader reads both outgoing and incoming pages during `AnimatePresence` overlap. - `aria-hidden` hides content visually from screen readers but focus still enters it because `inert` was omitted. - SPA route changes silently occur without title update, focus movement, or announcement. - Duplicate `main-content` IDs exist during transition overlap. - Reduced-motion implementation removes animation but causes blank or unstable route presentation. --- ## References - WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ - WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/ - WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions - WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order - WCAG 2.4.2 Page Titled - WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - framer-motion `useReducedMotion()` hook documentation: use the project’s installed framer-motion version as implementation reference.
